file_insights.primary_responsibility: Multi-tenant context resolution and organization loading middleware

patterns_discovered.description: In-memory organization cache with 5-minute TTL using Map structure
patterns_discovered.note: Explicitly marked as temporary, designed for future Redis migration

patterns_discovered.description: Middleware designed to chain after authMiddleware, with clear extraction of user context
patterns_discovered.benefit: Separation of concerns between authentication and tenant resolution

patterns_discovered.description: Dual middleware approach with required (tenantMiddleware) and optional (optionalTenantMiddleware) versions
patterns_discovered.use_case: Supports both protected routes requiring tenant context and public routes with optional personalization

patterns_discovered.description: Three different ways to resolve organization: by user context, by slug parameter, or by header
patterns_discovered.flexibility: Handles SaaS multi-tenant patterns including public routes and platform admin scenarios

patterns_discovered.description: Status-specific validation with distinct exception types for different failure modes
Request type hierarchy with TenantRequest and OptionalTenantRequest interfaces
patterns_discovered.benefit: Type-safe access to organization context in route handlers

gotchas_discovered.description: Developers must manually call invalidateOrganizationCache when organization data changes
gotchas_discovered.impact: Stale organization data if cache invalidation is forgotten
gotchas_discovered.mitigation: Document cache invalidation requirement in service layer implementations

gotchas_discovered.description: Super admin and platform admin users without organizationId require X-Organization-Id header
gotchas_discovered.impact: API consumers must know to provide header for platform users
gotchas_discovered.mitigation: Clear documentation of header requirement for platform operations

gotchas_discovered.description: DELETED status throws tenantNotFound rather than tenantAccessDenied, potentially masking deletion from unauthorized users
gotchas_discovered.impact: Subtle security implication - behavior differs from SUSPENDED status
gotchas_discovered.design_intent: Likely intentional to hide existence of deleted organizations

gotchas_discovered.description: Simple Map-based cache will not work in multi-instance deployments
gotchas_discovered.required_migration: Must implement Redis cache before scaling horizontally

gotchas_discovered.gotcha: No cache pre-warming or background refresh
gotchas_discovered.description: Cache only updated on-demand, no background refresh of frequently accessed organizations
gotchas_discovered.impact: Potential cache misses for popular organizations under high concurrency

approach_outcome.description: Created comprehensive multi-tenant context middleware with dual-mode support
key_achievements: Implemented required tenantMiddleware for protected routes
Provided optionalTenantMiddleware for public routes with optional personalization
Added slug-based resolution for public-facing routes
Implemented organization caching with TTL management
Provided helper functions for organization loading and cache management
Comprehensive type definitions for type-safe request handling
Clear error handling with specific exception types
Well-documented with extensive JSDoc comments and examples
design_decisions: Middleware composition after authMiddleware for dependency on authenticated user
Separate optional middleware variant to avoid failing on public routes
In-memory cache as temporary solution with clear migration path to Redis
Header-based organization override for platform admin scenarios
Status-based filtering to exclude deleted/suspended organizations

recommendations.recommendation: Implement Redis cache integration before production deployment
recommendations.details: Replace Map-based cache with Redis to support distributed deployments
Document how tenantMiddleware must follow authMiddleware and how to use TenantRequest types

recommendations.recommendation: Add cache invalidation hooks to organization update operations
recommendations.details: Ensure invalidateOrganizationCache is called after any organization update
Test middleware composition, cache behavior, status validation, and error scenarios
Pre-load frequently accessed organizations on server startup or background refresh

recommendations.recommendation: Add rate limiting for organization resolution failures
recommendations.details: Prevent enumeration attacks via slug guessing or invalid header manipulation

recommendations.recommendation: Add logging for tenant context resolution
recommendations.details: Log cache hits/misses, organization not found scenarios, and status validation failures

recommendations.recommendation: Document X-Organization-Id header requirement in API specification
recommendations.details: Clearly specify when and how platform users should provide organization header
